title: The Twelve-Factor App
subtitle: A Methodology for Building Modern, Cloud-Native Applications
author: Adam Wiggins
excerpt: A concise methodology for building software-as-a-service applications that are portable, resilient, and cloud-native — covering twelve principles from codebase management to admin processes.
keyIdeas: Strict separation of config from code
Disposability — fast startup and graceful shutdown
keyTakeaways: A twelve-factor app is a methodology, not a framework — applicable to any language or stack
Each factor addresses a specific weakness in traditional application deployment
The principles enable maximum portability across cloud providers and environments
whoShouldRead: Application developers deploying to cloud platforms
Anyone new to cloud-native application development
whoShouldSkip: Developers working on embedded or desktop-only applications
Those seeking language-specific tutorials rather than principles
Readers already deeply familiar with cloud-native patterns
